data.title: Add template selection gate to onboarding SKILL.md when framework is unknown
data.description:
## Description

Modify `plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md` to add a template selection gate after Phase 1 auto-detection.

### Implementation
- After Phase 1 runs project-detect.sh/detect-stack.sh, check if framework type is "unknown"
- If unknown: load template registry via parse-template-registry.sh, present numbered menu of available templates
- Handle "decline all" → proceed to existing manual flow unchanged
- Handle template selection → route to appropriate install method based on registry install_method field
- If registry is missing/malformed: gate is invisible — proceed to manual flow with warning
- Verify projects with recognized framework do NOT see the template menu

### File Impact
- MODIFY: `plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md` (PROTECTED FILE — requires user approval per CLAUDE.md rule 18)

### TDD Exemption
test-exempt: Unit exemption criterion 1 — skill file contains no conditional executable logic (it's a workflow prompt document). Changes are behavioral guidance for the LLM agent, not executable code.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `bash tests/run-all.sh` passes (exit 0)
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] SKILL.md contains template selection gate section
  Verify: grep -q "template.*selection\|template.*menu\|template.*gate" plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md
- [ ] Gate checks for unknown framework
  Verify: grep -q "unknown" plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md
- [ ] Gate references parse-template-registry.sh
  Verify: grep -q "parse-template-registry" plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md
- [ ] Decline option documented
  Verify: grep -qi "decline\|skip\|no template" plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md
